title: Zero protection for customers, Revolut always sides with the merchant
text:
I am beyond frustrated with Revolut's complete lack of buyer protection. They market themselves as a modern banking alternative, but when things go wrong with a merchant, they leave you completely stranded.
Most recently, I paid €33 for an Express Shipping service that was guaranteed to arrive at my home in 2 working days with a signature required. Instead, the service was a total failure: it took 6 days to arrive and was dumped at a local shop without any attempt to deliver to my house or get my signature.
Even though I have proof that the service I paid for was not provided, Revolut has been difficult and dismissive about the dispute.
This isn't the first time, either. A couple of years ago, I bought an online course costed me €250 where the seller failed to deliver what was promised. I never received the course materials, yet when I raised a dispute and provided the details, Revolut refused to help me. After they declined the claim, they simply ignored my follow-up messages.
It seems Revolut is happy to take your money, but the moment you need them to step in and investigate a clear breach of contract or a failed delivery, they hide behind automated bots and excuses. If you value your money and want a bank that actually protects you from bad sellers, stay away from Revolut.

title: I’ve been using Revolut for a while…
text:
I’ve been using Revolut for a while now, and honestly, their service stands out as one of the most reliable and convenient financial platforms I’ve tried. Everything from account setup to daily usage is smooth and intuitive. The app is fast, easy to navigate, and packed with useful features like instant notifications, budgeting tools, and seamless currency exchange.

What really makes Revolut shine is how efficient and modern their service feels. Transfers are quick, international payments are straightforward, and the transparency in fees is refreshing. I also appreciate the level of control they give users—being able to freeze cards instantly, manage spending, and track everything in real time adds a strong sense of security.

Customer support has been responsive when needed, and overall the experience feels very professional. If you're looking for a smart, flexible, and forward-thinking banking solution, Revolut is definitely worth it.

title: revolut is fine unless you are a low…
text:
revolut is fine unless you are a low profile client. We had yesterday a life risky moment where we had to dp
a payment by crypto of 47500 USD, in the middle of nowhere - and they blocked it, asking questions in front of all involved parties. Asked 25 times the same questions, asked to provide nonsense documents in a heavy situation about life and death. They kept blocking it while asking more questions. All the money on revolut above 15k is at risk. I tried to transfer all funds and guess what: BLOCKED again. 

This review is not for the typical standard traveller, backpacker or youngster. This is for all the people searching answers regarding high networth solutions. 

Revolut is definitively the wrong bank for such transactions. 

another example was on the 13/04/2026, where we made a transaction to the goverment / immigration in a country in africa as a team of 5. All transactions went trough except mine, cause revolut placed the receiver as „merchant“, which leaded to:
- pending visa status
- hotel cancellation
- private jet change if departure date with a fee of 38k USD 

and guess what: revolut dont care at all. 
The automated in-app chat stuff is absolute trash and not personal at all even if theres a human chatting.

Uncool. 
Deleting the „bank“ soon.

title: Very bad experience.
text:
Very bad experience.

I have had my account for years, I referred a friend and tried to set up a new account. The entire process has changed and you now are required to purchase a card to finish the account set up. (Ridiculous). I thought I may have clicked on the wrong option so started again after speaking with support about doing this and being told if we used the link the referral would still work.

Account was set up, all steps completed, no referral gift. Turns out, as  the process was restarted we did not qualify even though this was minutes apart, there was no exception. 

Then found out I could not use the account for the intended use of a joint account as the accounts were on different platforms. Was originally told the migration would take a few weeks. 

Asked again today to be told this would now take 2-3 months. Customer service was friendly and support bot works well but very unhappy with the entire process and felt more could have been done in way of compensation of my time due to internal issues.

title: I’ve been using Revolut for a while…
text:
I’ve been using Revolut for a while now, and honestly, it’s one of the most convenient banking apps I’ve ever tried. The interface is super user-friendly, and everything—from sending money to tracking expenses—is fast and seamless.

What I really love is the ability to hold and exchange multiple currencies, which makes it perfect for traveling or sending money abroad. Transfers are quick, and the fees are very transparent compared to traditional banks. 

The app also offers great features like budgeting tools, savings vaults, and instant notifications, which help me stay in control of my finances at all times.

Overall, Revolut feels modern, efficient, and designed for how people actually manage money today. I’d definitely recommend it to anyone looking for a flexible and innovative banking solution.
